text: Intraoperative TE showed a severe mitral insufficiency with multiple sites of regurgitation, as confirmed in 3D ecoreconstruction, which showed the A2P2 prolapse causing a non-contact of A1P1.
In the present case, the patient presented a prolapse of A2P2.
with A1P1 substantially normal and a prolapse of A3P3.
Hydrodynamic test allowed to confirm the preoperative findings and the intraoperative identification of regurgitation sites.
usually enabling the characterization of main, secondary and tertiary jets, if present.
The dominant jet was firstly approached, in this case using a 5-opraline for the approximation of A2P2, followed by correction of the jet of lesser entity.
After inspection of A3P3 prolapse, a 5-opralein stitch was placed to suture the edges of these scollops.
A hydrodynamic test showed a repaired mitral valve perfectly continent.
After that, a 5-0 praline was used to reinforce the A2-P2 stitch using a autologous pericardial pledges.
The valve was again tested, and once again, it showed to be perfectly continent.
The hydrodynamic test was repeated, after placing a 36 physio 2 mitral rim.
